records.ety: [LL. itinerans, -antis, p. pr. of itinerare to make a journey, fr. L. iter, itineris, a walk, way, journey. See Errant, Issue.]

defs: Passing or traveling about a country; going or preaching on a circuit; wandering; not settled; as, an itinerant preacher; an itinerant peddler.
quotes: The king's own courts were then itinerant, being kept in the king's palace, and removing with his household in those royal progresses which he continually made.

defs: One who travels from place to place, particularly a preacher; one who is unsettled.
quotes: Glad to turn itinerant, To stroll and teach from town to town.
